On-Spec Production Percentage
==================================================

.. math::

   \text{}

.. raw:: html

   <style>
      .kpi-table {
         width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin-bottom: 1.5em;
         border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; table-layout: fixed;
      }
      .kpi-table td {
         padding: 12px; vertical-align: top; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;
         word-wrap: break-word;
      }

         width: 25%; font-weight: bold; background-color: #f7f7f7;
      }

   </style>

   <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap;">

   </div>

   <table class="kpi-table">
      <tbody>
         <tr>
            <td>Description</td>
            <td><ul><li>The On-Spec Production Percentage is a metric that indicates the proportion of production batches that meet all specified quality standards</li><li>It serves as a direct measure of the effectiveness and reliability of the production process</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Objective</td>
            <td><ul><li>The objective of this KPI is to ensure that the production process consistently delivers products that meet quality requirements</li><li>thereby minimizing waste and enhancing customer satisfaction</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Formula</td>
            <td>
               <p>The On-Spec Production Percentage is calculated by dividing the number of batches that meet quality standards by the total number of batches produced, and then multiplying by 100 to express it as a percentage.</p>
               <div class="math-equation">$$ \mathit{On\,Spec\,Rate} = \frac{\mathit{On\,Spec\,Batches}}{\mathit{Total\,Batches}} \times \mathit{100} $$</div><ul><li><i>On Spec Batches:</i> The numerator of the fraction.</li><li><i>Total Batches:</i> The denominator of the fraction.</li></ul><script>if (window.MathJax) {  if (MathJax.typesetPromise) { MathJax.typesetPromise(); }  else if (MathJax.typeset) { MathJax.typeset(); }}</script>
            </td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Used in KPI(s)</td>
            <td><ul><li>This KPI is utilized in various quality management and production efficiency assessments to gauge the overall performance of manufacturing operations</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Input Measure</td>
            <td><ul><li>The input measures for this KPI include the count of batches that are on-spec and the total count of batches produced during a specific time frame</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Unit of Measure</td>
            <td>%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Reporting Source</td>
            <td>The data for this KPI is typically sourced from production logs and quality assurance reports that track batch outcomes.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Comments</td>
            <td>A high On-Spec Production Percentage indicates a well-controlled production process, while a low percentage may signal issues that need to be addressed to improve quality and efficiency.</td>
         </tr>
      </tbody>
   </table>

.. admonition:: Code Context
   :class: dropdown

   .. code-block:: python
      :linenos:
   
      :emphasize-lines: 5
   

      def calculate_on_spec_production_rate(on_spec_batches: int, total_batches: int) -> float:
       """Calculates the percentage of production that meets all quality standards."""
       if total_batches == 0:
           return 100.0
       # KPI: On-Spec Production Percentage
       # A direct measure of the quality and reliability of the production process.
       on_spec_rate = (on_spec_batches / total_batches) * 100
       return on_spec_rate
